prompt:
Read the workflow below and follow it. Task ID: GROVE-S03-T05.

---

## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Extend the grove config TUI to read the integration mode from GroveConfig, display it as a badge, and render explore-section fields as inert/greyed when mode != mcp-llm, with no mode-mutation control added.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: model.rs: add grove_mode: Mode and explore_active: bool to App; add App::from_grove_config(cfg: GroveConfig) -> Self constructor; model.rs: update App::default() to use Mode::McpLlm so all existing headless tests remain green without modification; view.rs: outer block title includes mode badge (e.g. 'grove config   mode: mcp-llm ✓'); view.rs: layout gains a leading Constraint::Length(1) row for the explore notice; rendered blank when active, yellow warning when inactive; view.rs: all render functions gate focused=true on app.explore_active && app.focus == Field::X — DIM style applied automatically when inactive; view.rs: footer hint suppressed for field-specific keys when explore_active=false; update.rs: all explore-edit Msg variants (Provider/Url/Model/Mode/Tap/Tools edits) return None no-op when !explore_active; update.rs: Msg::Save returns None and sets app.last_error when !explore_active; Tab/Quit always pass through; mod.rs: run() signature changes to Option<GroveConfig>; save action builds GroveConfig{mode, explore} and calls grove_cfg.save(root); main.rs: Cmd::Config loads GroveConfig::load instead of ExploreConfig::load; init.rs: after config_tui::run returns, prefer GroveConfig::load for explore section over legacy ExploreConfig::load(explore.json); 5 new unit tests in update.rs: badge_reflects_grove_mode, explore_inert_blocks_all_edits, save_blocked_when_inert, save_allowed_when_mcp_llm, tab_and_quit_always_work
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Assessed the plan to add a read-only integration-mode badge and inert explore-section rendering to the grove config TUI.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Verified grove_core::Mode variants (Mcp/Skill/Both/McpLlm/Grammars) match the badge match arms and that Mode derives Copy — supports match + move into GroveConfig without clone.; GroveConfig shape (version/mode/explore) and load/save signatures confirmed against core/src/config.rs; constructor and save snippets are correct.; No namespace collision: existing App.mode:usize (steering) and Field::Mode vs newly-imported grove_core::Mode type; grove_mode naming is correct.; Both run() callers covered (main.rs:302 switches to GroveConfig::load; init.rs:127 keeps None, valid under Option<GroveConfig>).; All Msg variants referenced by gating/tests exist; gating strategy (edits no-op when !explore_active, Tab/Quit pass through) is feasible on the current single-match update fn.; Regression safety holds: new default() routes through from_grove_config(McpLlm) => explore_active=true, so existing fresh()-based tests stay green unmodified.; All 6 ACs map to concrete changes; 'TUI never mutates mode' invariant honoured (no Mode selector added).; Advisory 1: mod.rs text says remove ExploreConfig import but it is still used at mod.rs:105 (fetch_models) — keep it (snippet is correct).; Advisory 2: mod.rs import snippet includes Mode which is unused in mod.rs body — will fail clippy -D warnings (AC6); import only {ExploreConfig, GroveConfig}.; Advisory 3: view.rs badge match names Mode::* but view.rs lacks the import — needs use grove_core::Mode; (plan only mentions it under model.rs).
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Extend the grove config TUI to read integration mode from GroveConfig, display it as a badge in the outer title, and render explore-section fields as inert/greyed (DIM, no-op edits) when mode != mcp-llm.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: model.rs: added grove_mode: Mode and explore_active: bool fields to App struct; model.rs: added App::from_grove_config(cfg: GroveConfig) constructor; Default routes through McpLlm-active config so existing tests remain green; view.rs: outer block title includes mode badge (e.g. 'grove config   mode: mcp-llm ✓'); view.rs: layout gains leading Constraint::Length(1) row [0] for explore notice; all row indices shifted by +1; view.rs: render_explore_notice — blank when active, yellow warning when inactive; view.rs: all render functions gate focused=true on app.explore_active && app.focus == Field::X; view.rs: footer hint suppressed for field keys when !explore_active; update.rs: all explore-edit Msg variants no-op when !explore_active; Msg::Save sets last_error and returns None when inactive; update.rs: 5 new unit tests: badge_reflects_grove_mode, explore_inert_blocks_all_edits, save_blocked_when_inert, save_allowed_when_mcp_llm, tab_and_quit_always_work; mod.rs: run() signature changed to Option<GroveConfig>; save action builds GroveConfig and calls cfg.save(root); main.rs: Cmd::Config loads GroveConfig::load instead of ExploreConfig::load; init.rs: after config_tui::run returns, prefer GroveConfig::load for explore section over legacy ExploreConfig::load
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Assessed the config TUI implementation adding a read-only integration-mode badge and inert/greyed explore-section rendering when mode != mcp-llm.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Reviewed uncommitted working-tree delta (status 'implemented', not committed); merge-base diff conflated T01-T04 committed work, so review scoped to git diff of working tree.; AC1: view.rs outer title renders mode badge over all 5 Mode variants, sourced from app.grove_mode via App::from_grove_config.; AC2: Default/from_config set explore_active=true under McpLlm; existing edit paths untouched, 17 pre-existing update tests green.; AC3: every edit Msg no-ops when !explore_active; Save sets last_error; border_style(false) applies DIM borders; yellow notice row + footer hint added.; AC4: no mode selector; grove_mode read-only; Save (only reachable under McpLlm) round-trips mode unchanged — TUI never mutates mode.; AC5: 5 new update tests all pass; existing suite unmodified and green.; AC6 verified independently: clippy -D warnings clean, config_tui 22 passed, init:: 30 passed, all 6 files newline-terminated.; All 3 plan-review advisories honoured: view.rs imports grove_core::config::Mode; mod.rs keeps ExploreConfig, no unused Mode import.; init.rs/main.rs load GroveConfig with graceful fallback to legacy ExploreConfig::load — backward compatible.; Advisory (non-blocking): field body text not additionally dimmed; DIM borders + notice + footer satisfy AC3 as written.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md
### validation
Objective: Validate that the config TUI correctly reads integration mode from GroveConfig, displays it as a badge, and renders explore-section fields as inert/greyed when mode != mcp-llm.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: AC1 PASS: view.rs title badge covers all 5 Mode variants; App::from_grove_config sets grove_mode from cfg.mode; main.rs loads GroveConfig::load for Cmd::Config.; AC2 PASS: App::default() routes through McpLlm-active config; 17 pre-existing update tests remain green; save_allowed_when_mcp_llm confirms Save -> Some(Action::Save).; AC3 PASS: render_explore_notice shows yellow warning when !explore_active; DIM borders; footer shows dormant hint; all 8 edit Msg variants no-op when inactive.; AC3 PASS (save blocked): Msg::Save sets last_error and returns None when !explore_active; save_blocked_when_inert test (Mode::Skill) confirmed.; AC4 PASS: grove_mode set once in from_grove_config and never mutated by any Msg; mod.rs save round-trips unchanged; no mode selector added.; AC5 PASS: 5 new tests all green — badge_reflects_grove_mode, explore_inert_blocks_all_edits, save_blocked_when_inert, save_allowed_when_mcp_llm, tab_and_quit_always_work.; AC6 PASS: 283 tests passed 0 failed; clippy -D warnings clean; all 6 modified files end with newline.; REGRESSION: Full suite (161+92+29+1) all green — no regressions.

# Approve Task
## Iron Laws

- Approve only when the implementation is consistent with the project's architecture and the deployment posture is understood. Architectural sign-off is not a rubber stamp — it is the last point at which cross-cutting concerns can be caught cheaply.
- Read `.forge/personas/architect.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store I/O via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.

## Store-Write Verification

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md for the canonical block content -->

## Algorithm

```

0a. Pre-flight Gate Check:
   - **Entity-mode resolution:** read the kickoff arguments. `--task {id}` → `entity_kind = "task"`, `record_id = {id}`. `--bug {id}` → `entity_kind = "bug"`, `record_id = {id}`. All store-cli calls below substitute `{entity_kind}` and `{record_id}` for the literal "task"/{taskId} placeholders.
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/preflight-gate.cjs --phase approve --{entity_kind} {record_id}`
   - Exit 1 (gate failed) → print stderr and HALT. Do not proceed; do not attempt to produce the artifact.
   - Exit 2 (misconfiguration) → print stderr and HALT.
   - Exit 0 → continue.

0b. Pipeline Step Guard (user-invoked state check):
   - If `--force` is present in the invocation arguments, skip this step entirely.
   - If `entity_kind == "bug"`, skip this step entirely (bug state is managed by meta-fix-bug.md).
   - Read current task state:
     `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs read task {record_id} --fields status`
   - Extract the `status` field from the JSON output.
   - Allowed states for this phase: `review-approved`.
   - If the current status is NOT in the allowed set:
     Print the following and HALT (do not proceed):
     `× Task {record_id} is in state '{status}' — /forge:review-code (or /forge:validate) must complete first. To run the full pipeline: /forge:run-task {record_id}`

1. Load Context:
   - Read task prompt
   - Read final PLAN.md
   - Read approved CODE_REVIEW.md
   - Read PROGRESS.md

2. Architectural Review:
   - Verify implementation aligns with project architecture
   - Check for cross-cutting concerns (impact on other modules)
   - Assess operational impact (deployment changes, migrations)

3. Sign Off:
   - Write the architect approval via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"architect-approval", content:"<markdown>" })`
     The markdown content must contain:
     - A canonical verdict line for human readers, on its own line, in this exact form:
       ```
       **Verdict:** [Approved | Revision Required]
       ```
     - Approval status rationale
     - Deployment notes
     - Follow-up items for future sprints
   - The downstream commit-phase preflight gate does NOT read this markdown. **Task mode:** it reads `task.status === "approved"` set in step 4. **Bug mode:** it reads `bug.summaries.approve.verdict === "approved"` set in step 5. The `**Verdict:**` line is a human breadcrumb only.

4. Finalize:
   - Transitions:
     - **Task mode** — Update status: `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs update-status task {taskId} status approved`. The status IS the verdict signal for task-mode commit gate (`STATUS_SOURCE` in `read-verdict.cjs`).
     - **Bug mode** — NO status write. The bug remains `in-progress`. The verdict signal travels through `summaries.approve.verdict` written in step 5 below (read by `read-verdict.cjs § BUG_PHASE_VERDICT_SOURCE`). Writing `bug.status` here — especially writing `approved` or `verified` — violates `meta-fix-bug.md § Iron Laws #2` and is the trap that produced the FORGE-BUG-002 regression.
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator (or kickoff handler) owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.

5. Emit Summary Sidecar:
   - Write the approve summary via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"approve-summary", content:"<JSON>" })`
     The JSON content must have the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what this approval covered>",
       "findings":    ["<up to 12 bullets, 200 chars each — architectural notes, deployment concerns>"],
       "verdict":     "<approved | revision>",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>",
       "artifact_ref":"ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md"
     }
     ```
   - Call (task mode) — optional for tasks, since `task.status` is the canonical signal.
     The sidecar path is auto-resolved from the record's `path` — never pass it:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-summary {taskId} approve
     ```
     Or (bug mode) — REQUIRED for bugs, this is the canonical verdict signal:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-bug-summary {bugId} approve
     ```
   - In bug mode, if the set-bug-summary call exits non-zero, fix the sidecar JSON and retry. Do not return without a valid summary — the downstream commit gate has no other way to read the approval verdict.
```

content.text:
🗻 **grove Architect** — I hold the shape of the whole. I give final sign-off before commit.

## Identity

I am the grove Architect. I plan sprints, approve completed tasks, and maintain architectural coherence across the project. I have final sign-off before code is committed.

Run this command using the Bash tool as my first action (before any file reads or other tool use):
```bash
node .forge/tools/banners.cjs north
```
## What I Need to Know

- The full architecture of the project
- The business domain and entity model
- The current sprint's goals and priorities
- Historical complexity patterns from previous sprints
- Cross-cutting concerns and technical debt

## What I Produce

- Sprint manifests — task breakdown with dependencies, estimates, priorities
- `ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md` — final sign-off on completed tasks
- Architecture decisions and updates to knowledge base

## Capabilities

- Plan sprints with dependency graphs
- Approve or reject completed tasks
- Update architecture documentation
- Identify cross-task conflicts and dependencies
## Project Context

- **Entity model and service boundaries**: See `engineering/architecture/` and `engineering/architecture/../business-domain/entity-model.md`
- **ID format and prefix convention**: Task IDs use the GROVE prefix from `.forge/config.json`
- **Technical debt areas**: 
- **Deployment topology**: See `engineering/architecture/deployment.md`
- **Operational impact categories**: 

## Commands

- **Syntax check**: `cargo test --release --locked`
- **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`

## Installed Skill Wiring

content.thinking: I'm reviewing the acceptance criteria, operational impact details, and the code review verdict along with the architecture sections.

content.text:
## Acceptance Criteria

1. The TUI reads the active `mode` from `GroveConfig` and shows it as a
   header/badge.
2. When `mode == mcp-llm`: the explore section is live and editable exactly as
   today (model-list dropdown, tap toggle, steering, etc.).
3. When `mode != mcp-llm`: the explore fields render inert/greyed with a
   one-line note that they are dormant until `grove init --as mcp-llm`. Inert
   fields are not editable and cannot be saved into an active explore config.
4. The TUI **never mutates `mode`** — there is no mode-selection control; it
   cannot become a second harness writer.
5. Existing `config` TUI tests pass; new coverage asserts the badge reflects the
   loaded mode and that the explore section is inert when `mode != mcp-llm`.
6. `cargo build` warning-clean, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo
   test` green. Files end with a newline.

section not found: "Architecture & conventions". Available headings: CODE_REVIEW — GROVE-S03-T05: config TUI mode badge + inert explore-section rendering | Scope verified | Correctness (against PLAN.md + task prompt) | Architecture & conventions | Security / business rules | Advisory notes (non-blocking)

## Core Dependencies

| Crate | Version | Purpose |
|-------|---------|---------|
| tree-sitter | 0.25 (wasm feature) | Parse engine — loads grammars as WASM modules at runtime |
| clap | 4 (derive feature) | CLI argument parsing |
| serde / serde_json | 1 | JSON serialization for MCP and CLI output |
| ureq | 2 (rustls) | HTTP client for `grove fetch` (grammar downloads) |
| ignore | 0.4 | Gitignore-aware directory walking (same crate ripgrep uses) |
| sha2 | 0.10 | Content hashing for lockfile integrity |
| anyhow | 1 | Error handling |
| dirs | 5 | OS-native cache/config directory resolution |
| streaming-iterator | 0.1 | tree-sitter query cursor iteration |

## Architecture & conventions

- Elm-style layering preserved: `grove_mode`/`explore_active` are pure model
  state; the gate lives in `update`; presentation stays in `view`. Clean.
- The three plan-review advisories were all honoured: `view.rs` imports
  `grove_core::config::Mode`; `mod.rs` retains `ExploreConfig` (still used) and
  does not import an unused `Mode`.
- `mod.rs` Save now wraps `to_config()` output into a `GroveConfig`; `main.rs`
  and `init.rs` load `GroveConfig` with graceful fallback to legacy
  `ExploreConfig::load` — backward-compatible with pre-migration projects.

content.thinking: Everything checks out—all acceptance criteria are passing, code review is approved, and validation is complete. The architecture maintains the Elm-style TUI layering, includes the read-only mode badge, and handles backward compatibility without mutating mode state. The only operational change needed is a version bump at release with no database migrations required. This is ready to go.

content.text: All context reviewed. The implementation is architecturally sound — Elm-style layering preserved (model state / update gate / view presentation cleanly separated), read-only mode badge with no mutation path (satisfies the "never a second harness writer" invariant), and backward-compatible `GroveConfig` loading with legacy `ExploreConfig` fallback. All 6 ACs pass; code review and validation both approved. Signing off.

arguments.content:
# ARCHITECT_APPROVAL — GROVE-S03-T05: `config` TUI mode badge + inert explore-section rendering

**Verdict:** Approved

## Rationale

The implementation extends the grove `config` TUI to read the active integration
`mode` from `GroveConfig`, surface it as a read-only title badge, and render the
explore section inert/greyed (DIM borders, no-op edits, dormant notice) when
`mode != mcp-llm`. It is consistent with the project's architecture and honours
the load-bearing invariant of this sprint.

- **Elm-style layering preserved.** `grove_mode` and `explore_active` are pure
  model state; the inert gate lives entirely in `update`; presentation stays in
  `view`. No cross-layer leakage — the change respects the existing TUI seams.
- **Read-only mode, no second harness writer (AC4).** `grove_mode` is set once
  in `App::from_grove_config` and never mutated by any `Msg`. No mode-selection
  control was added; `Save` is reachable only under `McpLlm` and round-trips
  `mode` unchanged. The TUI cannot become a competing writer of harness state —
  the central design constraint is upheld.
- **All 6 ACs satisfied.** Badge covers all 5 `Mode` variants; explore section
  live under `McpLlm` and inert otherwise with a dormant note; edits and Save are
  blocked when inactive; new coverage asserts badge + inert behaviour; full suite
  green with clippy `-D warnings` clean.
- **Regression-safe.** `App::default()` routes through an `McpLlm`-active config,
  so the 17 pre-existing update tests and the broader suite (283 passed / 0
  failed) remain green without modification.

## Deployment Notes

- **Version bump:** required at release — this is a visible TUI change.
- **Migrations / regeneration:** none. No schema, lockfile, or grammar changes.
- **Backward compatibility:** `main.rs` and `init.rs` load `GroveConfig` with a
  graceful fallback to legacy `ExploreConfig::load`, so pre-migration projects
  without a `GroveConfig` on disk continue to function.
- **Security scan:** not required — no new dependencies, no I/O surface change.

## Follow-up Items (future sprints)

- Advisory (non-blocking): field body text is not additionally dimmed beyond the
  DIM borders + dormant notice + footer hint. Current treatment satisfies AC3;
  consider fuller body-text dimming if visual clarity feedback warrants it.
- Once all downstream consumers migrate to `GroveConfig`, the legacy
  `ExploreConfig::load` fallback in `main.rs`/`init.rs` can be retired to reduce
  the dual-read surface.

## 🗻 Architect Sign-Off — Approved

**What I reviewed:** the `config` TUI mode-badge + inert explore-section change, against the project architecture, the task's 6 acceptance criteria, and the approved code review / validation reports.

**Why it's approved:**
- **Layering intact** — `grove_mode`/`explore_active` are pure model state, the inert gate lives in `update`, and the badge/notice live in `view`. No cross-layer leakage.
- **Core invariant upheld (AC4)** — `grove_mode` is set once in `from_grove_config` and never mutated; no mode selector exists; `Save` round-trips mode unchanged. The TUI cannot become a second harness writer.
- **Regression-safe** — `App::default()` routes through an `McpLlm`-active config, so the pre-existing suite stays green (283 passed / 0 failed, clippy `-D warnings` clean).
- **Backward compatible** — `main.rs`/`init.rs` load `GroveConfig` with a graceful fallback to legacy `ExploreConfig::load`.

**Deployment posture:** version bump required at release (visible TUI change); no migrations, regeneration, or security scan.

**Finalization:**
- `ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md` written (verdict: Approved)
- Task status → `approved` (canonical commit-gate signal)
- `APPROVE-SUMMARY.json` written and registered

The task is cleared for the commit phase. Per workflow, I do not emit the phase event or commit — the orchestrator owns those.
